content:
# Daily Cleaning Checklist

**Tool ID:** T011-01
**Block:** B011 (Cleaning Schedule Protocol)
**Domain:** Food Safety & Hygiene (D01_food_safety)
**Level:** L1
**Format:** Checklist
**Role:** All Staff
**Time Required:** Ongoing through shift (logging takes 1 min per task)
**Version:** 1.0

---
## Welcome

This is a foundational tool -- the kind that prevents problems before they start. It is designed to be picked up and used immediately, no training required.

Not another tick-box exercise. This checklist exists because cleaning standards slip gradually. A slightly sticky counter on Monday becomes an accepted baseline by Friday. This log creates a non-negotiable daily standard that is visible, accountable, and auditable.

**Built for All Staff working in Food Safety & Hygiene.**
Use it. Adapt it. Make it yours.

---

## Purpose

Printable A4 daily checklist listing every cleaning task by shift phase (opening, service, closing) with columns for task, method, product, initials, time, and manager sign-off. Provides documented evidence of cleaning compliance for EHO inspections.

This tool supports **Cleaning Schedule Protocol** (B011) and is designed to be practical, printable, and immediately useful in a working cafe environment.

---

## How to Use This Tool

**Who:** All Staff (everyone cleans; Shift Manager reviews)
**Time Required:** Tasks spread through the shift; logging takes under 1 minute per task
**Frequency:** Daily -- new sheet every day

**What You'll Need:**
- This checklist printed on A4, on clipboard in the kitchen
- Correct cleaning products (see COSHH register T011-05)
- Colour-coded cleaning cloths (blue for general, red for raw food areas, green for bar)
- Disposable gloves for chemical handling
- Pen for initialling

**Steps:**
1. Opening staff member starts a fresh sheet, filling in the date
2. Complete each task in the relevant shift phase
3. Initial and time-stamp each task as you complete it
4. Shift Manager reviews and countersigns at shift handover
5. Closing staff member completes all closing tasks and files the sheet

---

## Checklist

### Date: _______________ Day: _______________

### Opening Tasks (06:30-07:30)

| # | Task | Method / Product | Done | Time | Initials |
|---|------|-----------------|------|------|----------|
| 1 | Sanitise all food contact surfaces (prep bench, sandwich station) | Antibacterial spray, blue cloth, leave 30 sec contact time | ☐ | _____ | _____ |
| 2 | Clean and sanitise espresso machine drip tray and group heads | Backflush with Cafiza, wipe exterior with damp cloth | ☐ | _____ | _____ |
| 3 | Wipe down bar counter and till area | Antibacterial spray, blue cloth | ☐ | _____ | _____ |
| 4 | Check and restock handwash basins (soap, paper towels) | Visual check, restock from supplies cupboard | ☐ | _____ | _____ |
| 5 | Sweep kitchen floor | Broom, dustpan | ☐ | _____ | _____ |
| 6 | Empty and reline all bins (kitchen, bar, customer area) | Remove bag, tie, replace with new liner. Clean bin interior if soiled. | ☐ | _____ | _____ |
| 7 | Wipe customer tables and chairs | Antibacterial spray, clean cloth | ☐ | _____ | _____ |
| 8 | Clean glass display case (inside and outside) | Glass cleaner, lint-free cloth | ☐ | _____ | _____ |

### During Service Tasks (ongoing through the day)

| # | Task | Method / Product | Done | Time | Initials |
|---|------|-----------------|------|------|----------|
| 9 | Wipe down espresso machine steam wand after EVERY use | Damp cloth, purge steam | ☐ ongoing | -- | _____ |
| 10 | Clear and sanitise customer tables after each use | Antibacterial spray, clean cloth, clear crockery to wash area | ☐ ongoing | -- | _____ |
| 11 | Sanitise prep surfaces between different food types | Antibacterial spray, blue cloth, 30 sec contact time | ☐ ongoing | -- | _____ |
| 12 | Empty bins when 3/4 full (do not let overflow) | Remove, tie, replace. Wash hands after handling bins. | ☐ as needed | -- | _____ |
| 13 | Clean spills immediately (floor, counter, equipment) | Appropriate product for surface. Wet floor sign if needed. | ☐ as needed | -- | _____ |
| 14 | Rinse and sanitise Vitamix blender between uses | Blend hot water with drop of detergent, rinse, air dry | ☐ ongoing | -- | _____ |

### Closing Tasks (17:00-18:30)

| # | Task | Method / Product | Done | Time | Initials |
|---|------|-----------------|------|------|----------|
| 15 | Deep clean espresso machine (backflush, wipe, drip tray) | Cafiza backflush all groups. Remove and soak shower screens. Wipe body. Empty and clean drip tray. | ☐ | _____ | _____ |
| 16 | Clean and sanitise grinder hoppers and doser | Brush out retained grounds. Wipe exterior. Weekly: run Grindz tablets. | ☐ | _____ | _____ |
| 17 | Sanitise all food prep surfaces and chopping boards | Antibacterial spray, 30 sec contact time. Boards through dishwasher at 82C+. | ☐ | _____ | _____ |
| 18 | Clean and sanitise sinks (prep sink and handwash basin) | Cream cleaner, rinse, antibacterial spray | ☐ | _____ | _____ |
| 19 | Mop kitchen floor with sanitiser solution | Mop with food-safe floor sanitiser at correct dilution (check COSHH sheet T011-05) | ☐ | _____ | _____ |
| 20 | Mop cafe floor | Mop with floor cleaner. Wet floor signs until dry. | ☐ | _____ | _____ |
| 21 | Empty ALL bins, clean bin lids and surrounds | Remove bags, wipe bin interiors with antibacterial. Replace liners. | ☐ | _____ | _____ |
| 22 | Clean customer toilet (bowl, seat, taps, mirror, floor, restock) | Toilet cleaner for bowl. Antibacterial for all other surfaces. Restock paper and soap. | ☐ | _____ | _____ |
| 23 | Wipe down all door handles, light switches, and high-touch surfaces | Antibacterial spray, disposable cloth | ☐ | _____ | _____ |
| 24 | Final check: all surfaces clean, floors dry, bins empty, supplies stocked | Visual walkthrough by closing staff member | ☐ | _____ | _____ |

**Completion:**
- Date: _______________
- Opening completed by: _______________
- Closing completed by: _______________
- Shift Manager review: _______________
- Issues noted: _______________
- Follow-up needed: ☐ Yes ☐ No

---

## Common Risks

### Risk 1: Closing Tasks Rushed or Skipped Because Staff Want to Leave

**Why it happens:** It is 18:15, the closing barista has been on shift since 07:00, and they want to go home. Deep cleaning the espresso machine feels like it can wait until morning. But morning staff arrive to dirty equipment and a lower hygiene standard.
**How to prevent:** Build realistic closing times into the shift schedule. If closing takes 45 minutes, the shift ends at 18:30, not 18:00. Allocate specific closing tasks to specific people so nobody is doing everything. The Manager should spot-check closing quality at least twice per week.
**If it is already happening:** Add 15 minutes to the closing shift. Distribute tasks between closing team members. Consider a closing "buddy check" where each person verifies the other has completed their tasks.

### Risk 2: During-Service Cleaning Abandoned When It Gets Busy

**Why it happens:** During the 08:00-10:00 morning rush, clearing tables and wiping surfaces drops to zero priority. Customers sit at dirty tables, crumbs accumulate on the counter, and the steam wand gets caked with dried milk.
**How to prevent:** Assign a specific person to "floor duties" during rush periods. Even if everyone is on the bar, rotate one person off for 5 minutes every 30 minutes to clear, wipe, and reset. The steam wand must be wiped after every use -- this is non-negotiable, rush or not.
**If it is already happening:** The steam wand issue is the most urgent. Dried milk in the wand is both a hygiene risk and an equipment damage risk. Brief the team: wipe it every time, no exceptions. For table clearing, introduce a visible timer (phone alarm every 15 minutes) during peak periods.

### Risk 3: Wrong Cleaning Products Used on Food Contact Surfaces

**Why it happens:** Staff grab the glass cleaner (which contains ammonia) and spray it on the sandwich prep bench. Or they use the toilet cleaner on the bar counter. Using the wrong chemical on food contact surfaces can lead to chemical contamination of food.
**How to prevent:** Label every spray bottle clearly with its contents and where it should be used. Store food-safe cleaning products separately from non-food products. Brief staff on the COSHH register (T011-05) which lists every chemical and its correct use.
**If it is already happening:** Audit all cleaning products today. Remove any unlabelled bottles. Check that food-safe antibacterial spray is the ONLY product used on food contact surfaces.

---

## Customisation Notes

**This tool is a starting point, not a rulebook.**

**Independent / Specialty Cafe:** This checklist covers a typical medium-sized cafe. Remove tasks that do not apply (e.g., if you do not have a customer toilet) and add any that are specific to your layout.
**Chain / Franchise:** Your brand likely has a mandated cleaning schedule. Use this as a supplement or cross-reference. Ensure your brand-specific equipment (e.g., specific espresso machine model) cleaning procedures are included.
**Kiosk / Mobile:** Simplify to 10-12 essential tasks. In a kiosk, the entire clean-down may take 20 minutes. Focus on food contact surfaces, the espresso machine, and the bin.
**Bakery Cafe:** Add tasks for flour cleanup (flour dust settles on every surface), oven cleaning, and proving rack sanitisation.

**Making it yours:**
- Add or remove tasks to match your exact layout and equipment
- Adjust shift times to match your actual opening hours
- Specify your actual cleaning products by name and dilution rate
- Print a week supply every Monday and file completed sheets in the SFBB diary

The structure matters more than the specific fields. Keep the structure, change the content.

---

## Connected Resources

**Other tools in this block:**
- T011-02 -- Master Cleaning Schedule (L2, template)
- T011-03 -- Cleaning SOP Document (L2, SOP)
- T011-04 -- Weekly Cleaning Audit Scorecard (L2, scorecard)
- T011-05 -- COSHH Chemical Register (L1, tracker)

**Related blocks:**
- B009

**Parent block:** B011 -- Cleaning Schedule Protocol
**Module:** M01: Food Safety & Hygiene Compliance
---

*Tool Version 1.0 -- OIS Cafe v2*
*Built for cafe operators who value clarity, consistency, and craft.*
